source_jobs.title: UI/UX Designer - Robot Interfaces
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

In this role, you’ll work across the full stack to build intuitive, high-performance interfaces for real-time remote control of heavy machinery via novel user interfaces. It requires strong frontend skills for seamless user experiences and backend expertise to ensure a secure, scalable architecture. Daily tasks include prototyping data-rich visualizations, building pipelines for complex sensor data (e.g., LiDAR, geospatial, 3D), and optimizing user interactions. Rapid prototyping and iteration with users will be key. You’ll also help manage production environments, deploy applications, and apply best practices in networking, security, and deployment.



Responsibilities

 - Design and build intuitive, high-performance user interfaces for tasking, supervising, and remotely controlling autonomous robots and workflows

 - Develop real-time visualizations for complex sensor data, including LiDAR, 3D models, and geospatial information, with a focus on rapid prototyping and user feedback

 - Apply best practices in networking, authentication, and security to ensure low-latency, reliable communication between systems

 - Architect and maintain scalable, secure backend systems, ensuring efficient deployment and operation in production environments (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes)

 - Iterate quickly, collaborate cross-functionally, and optimize the full-stack experience to deliver robust and user-friendly solutions

 - Lead interdisciplinary teams, mentor new engineers, and help grow the team through hiring



Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field

 - 2+ years of professional or project experience as a full-stack or application developer, working across frontend, backend, and infrastructure

 - Proficiency in building interactive UIs using modern frameworks and platforms (e.g., React, Vue.js, Unity, Unreal, or equivalent)

 - A strong understanding of computer networking principles, including routing and protocols across the OSI model

 - Proficiency in developing software to run on Linux-based systems


Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience working with real-time data systems or low-latency communication protocols (e.g., WebRTC, gRPC)

 - Experience with data visualization, transformations, and working with 3D, geospatial, or LiDAR data

 - Experience developing for game engines (e.g., Unity, Un

source_jobs.title: Field Engineer - Heavy Civil & Excavation
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.


Job Overview
In this role, you will own on-site technical oversight, ensuring seamless coordination of operations, adherence to project specifications, and integration of advanced technologies like automation tools, tele-operations, and our proprietary Mission Control. The Field Engineer will also operate heavy equipment at a functional level to support project needs, collaborating closely with operators, estimators, and project managers to deliver high-quality results.



Responsibilities

 - Provide technical oversight during project execution, ensuring earthworks (e.g., excavation, grading, trenching) align with digital plans, specifications, budget and timeline

 - Coordinate on-site activities, including scheduling equipment and labor, to maintain project timelines and efficiency

 - Train, support, and manage operators in the effective use of automated and remote-controlled earth-moving equipment, ensuring optimal performance

 - Operate heavy equipment (e.g., excavators, bulldozers, graders) at a functional level to support team efforts during peak workloads or specific tasks

 - Monitor and troubleshoot machine control systems and teleoperations to ensure equipment performance and accuracy

 - Conduct quality control checks, such as soil compaction tests or grade verification, to meet engineering standards

 - Interpret and manage survey layout and staking

 - Maintain detailed daily logs and reports using mobile apps or project management software for progress tracking

 - Ensure strict compliance with safety regulations and industry standards, addressing the safety considerations of automated construction

 - Support site demobilization and final inspections, ensuring proper documentation and client handover



Basic Qualifications

 - 1-4 years in site prep / excavation, with proven exposure to field operations and technical coordination

 - Strong understanding of earthworks construction techniques, materials, and equipment

 - Experience operating heavy equipment required (functional proficiency, not expert level)

 - Bachelor’s degree in civil engineering, construction management, or related field; equivalent field experience considered



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Affinity with construction automation, robotics, or advanced technology integration in project management

 - Strong problem-solving abilities and adaptability to new technologies and c

source_jobs.title: Head of Finance
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

You will own and scale TerraFirma’s finance function. As our first dedicated finance hire, you will work directly with the Chief of Staff and cofounders to tighten our systems, controls, and operating cadence so the company stays fast while becoming financially precise.

This role is hands-on across accounting, reporting, finance operations, controls, strategic finance, fundraising support, and treasury. You will improve and automate existing workflows, implement lightweight controls where they matter, and build real-time visibility into cash, burn, runway, unit economics, and capital needs as we scale machines, deployments, and headcount.


Responsibilities

 - Accounting, reporting, and tax
   
   - Own GAAP and management reporting across the business
   
   - Lead month-end close and maintain accurate, audit-ready financials
   
   - Manage tax reporting, filings, payments, exemptions, R&D credits, and other credits

 - Finance operations and controls
   
   - Establish procurement, purchasing approvals, and right-sized internal controls
   
   - Own accounts payable, vendor payments, approvals, and tracking
   
   - Own accounts receivable, invoice generation, tracking, and collections
   
   - Manage employee reimbursements and expense policies
   
   - Support insurance applications, renewals, and compliance

 - Strategic finance and operating visibility
   
   - Build and run budgets, rolling forecasts, cash planning, burn tracking, and runway reporting
   
   - Develop unit economics, performance metrics, and cost structure analysis across machines, deployments, and operations
   
   - Partner with engineering, operations, and supply chain on capex planning and buy vs. lease decisions
   
   - Prepare board, investor, and leadership reporting

 - Financing and fundraising support
   
   - Support fundraising materials, projections, investor diligence, and ad hoc analysis for leadership
   
   - Manage data rooms for financing applications and diligence
   
   - Source and execute non-dilutive financing for equipment and materials
   
   - Build and manage lender relationships and financing diligence processes

 - Treasury and special projects
   
   - Manage cash visibility and support investment of excess cash
   
   - Support M&A and other strategic projects, as applicable


Basic Qualifications

 - 3 to 7 years in a high-ownership finance r

source_jobs.title: Head of Talent
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

You will take full ownership of talent acquisition at TerraFirma. As the primary driver of our hiring efforts, you will identify, engage, and close the engineers and operators capable of transforming a legacy industry. You will own recruiting end-to-end, from defining the hiring strategy with leadership to the tactical execution of sourcing, scheduling, and closing candidates. We are looking for someone who understands that the quality of our team is the single biggest predictor of our success, and who operates with the precision and urgency required to build a world-class organization.



Responsibilities

 - Manage the recruiting lifecycle end-to-end and serve as the point of contact for candidates from initial outreach through offer negotiation and onboarding

 - Partner directly with engineering and operations leadership to forecast hiring needs, define role requirements, and build a headcount plan that aligns with company milestones

 - Proactively identify and engage top 1% talent through non-traditional channels, map the market, and pursue the best engineers and operators across construction, software, robotics, and hardware

 - Run high-signal screens to evaluate technical competence and cultural alignment, maintaining our hiring bar and ensuring only the most capable candidates advance

 - Orchestrate complex onsite interview schedules at our Texas facilities and manage coordination and logistics to ensure a seamless, high-quality candidate experience

 - Analyze funnel data to identify bottlenecks, improve conversion rates, and reduce time-to-hire without sacrificing quality



Basic Qualifications

 - 3+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience in a technical or high-growth environment

 - Demonstrated ability to understand complex technical requirements and translate them into effective sourcing and screening strategies

 - Strong organizational skills with experience managing multiple high-priority roles simultaneously

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills with the ability to represent the company narrative compellingly to passive candidates



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience recruiting for hardware, robotics, manufacturing, or other “hard tech” companies such as aerospace, automotive, or defense

 - Experience as a founding recruiter or in an early-stage startup environment where you owned scheduling and coordina

source_jobs.title: Electromechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview
Design, prototype, and deploy rugged electromechanical systems that integrate precision mechanical design with robust electrical components. Develop enclosures, actuators, linkages, and wiring harnesses built for extreme conditions. Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to rapidly prototype, test, and refine systems for reliable real-world performance.



Responsibilities

 - Design mechanical systems with enclosures, actuators, linkages, mounts, and custom brackets for remote operation of heavy machinery

 - Rapidly prototype and iterate using 3D printing, CNC machining, and other in-house fabrication methods

 - Work with internal engineering teams, vendors, and manufacturers to ensure designs are feasible and cost-effective

 - Build and test electromechanical assemblies designed to survive vibration, impact, water ingress, and outdoor exposure.

 - Integrate wiring harnesses with mechanically secure routing, protection, and strain relief

 - Diagnose and repair mechanical and electrical components using standard tools and hands-on evaluation

 - Collaborate with vendors and fabricators to produce reliable, field-ready mechanical parts and electrical components

 - Maintain CAD models, mechanical drawings, and build documentation to support repeatable assembly



Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Electromechanical, Mechanical, or Mechatronics Engineering

 - Hands-on experience designing and assembling mechanical systems with actuators and sensors

 - Proficient in mechanical prototyping using 3D printing, machining, and basic fabrication tools

 - Familiarity with field deployment challenges like mechanical fatigue, waterproofing, and ruggedization

 - Ability to collaborate across disciplines with electrical and software teams



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience designing electromechanical systems for Drones, Electric Vehicles, Aircraft, or Robotics strongly preferred

 - Strong mechanical design of electronics enclosures for extreme environments

 - Experience with motion systems including brushless DC motors, multi-bar linkages, kinematics, basic microcontroller programming

 - Experience with structural analysis and material selection for outdoor, high-impact environments

 - Familiarity with sensor integration and other motion feedback components

 - Understanding of EMI shielding, thermal management, and protective enclosure desi

source_jobs.title: Perception Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

You will design and implement the perception systems that give our automated heavy machinery the awareness needed to operate effectively in complex, unstructured environments. This role combines high-level architecture decisions with hands-on algorithm development, hardware integration, and field testing. You will work across the perception stack, from integrating and calibrating advanced sensors to building real-time processing pipelines for images, point clouds, and other high-bandwidth data streams. Your work will power critical functions such as collision avoidance, motion planning, localization, and operator visualization.



Responsibilities

 - Integrate multi-spectral and TOF sensors into robotic systems with real-time processing constraints

 - Work with motion and trajectory planning teams to design and synthesize sensor systems to extract required signals and inputs

 - Implement algorithms to efficiently detect objects of interest from incoming data streams for the purposes of collision avoidance, motion planning, and trajectory planning

 - Stitch together and manipulate camera feeds and data from other sensors to effectively visualize the state of physical systems to human operators

 - Develop real-time pipelines for processing, sharing, and fusing data from multiple sensor sources

 - Train Machine Learning (ML) models on existing and custom datasets and support collection and labeling of data for additional training and validation

 - Develop simulation and visualization tools to aid in rapid prototyping of robotic software and control algorithms

 - Own the integration, calibration, and testing of sensors, both for proof-of-concept prototypes and production systems

 - Lead interdisciplinary teams, mentor new employees, and actively participate in the hiring process
   

Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Robotics, Mechanical Engineering, Physics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a closely related field

 - 2+ years of professional or project experience with robotic software development, involving implementation of real-time algorithms for perception and data processing pipelines

 - Demonstrated experience owning large-scale deliverables, leading teams, and driving product roadmaps


Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience working with safety-critical or real-time systems

 - Experience working with embedded applicatio

source_jobs.title: Embedded Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

In this role, you'll take hands-on ownership of the embedded hardware and software for our robots. You'll be responsible for both high-level design of custom hardware and low-level code implementation. Day-to-day work includes developing software for Linux-based systems with real-time constraints and microcontrollers for sensing and control. You'll lead platform bringup for new boards, develop drivers for new chips and sensors, and help shape TerraFirma’s software testing framework and standards.



Responsibilities

 - Design and architect firmware and low-level code for embedded systems and microcontrollers

 - Perform platform bringup for new hardware, including driver implementation, sensor integration, boot chain development, and basic networking

 - Own the integration, calibration, and testing of sensors both for proof-of-concept prototypes and production systems

 - Lead interdisciplinary teams, mentor new employees, and actively participate in the hiring process
   
   

Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a closely related field

 - 2+ years of professional or project experience with software and firmware development for embedded systems and microcontrollers

 - Demonstrated experience owning large scale deliverables, leading teams, and driving product roadmaps


Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience working with safety-critical or real-time systems

 - Experience working with embedded applications for robotics, autonomous vehicles, aerospace, automotive, or construction

 - Familiarity with platform and toolchain development for embedded systems

 - Experience building testing frameworks or designing CI/CD pipelines
   
   

Additional Requirements

 - Must be willing to work extended hours or weekends when needed to support critical deadlines or triage urgent deployment issues

 - Comfortable with working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX if not within commuting distance


Compensation & Benefits

Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience and education. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. Our total compensation package also includes equity in TerraFirma. Relocation packages are available to help cover moving costs, if applicable.

source_jobs.title: Senior Earthworks Estimator
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

You will prepare accurate cost estimates and plan for our earthworks projects, which involve advanced automation technologies. This role is crucial in ensuring that our projects are both feasible and profitable, supporting our mission to revolutionize construction.



Responsibilities

 - Prepare detailed project take-off and cost estimates for earthworks projects, including materials, labor, equipment, and other required elements

 - Review project plans, specifications, and other documents to understand the scope of work and identify cost drivers

 - Collaborate with engineers, project managers and business development managers to gather information necessary for accurate estimates

 - Develop estimation Standard Operating Procedures and templates that allow us to bid on projects in a lean, fast and low risk manner, all the while winning projects

 - Participate in site walk and customer interaction needed to scope and advance project bidding processes

 - Monitor project costs during construction to ensure they align with estimates and identify variances early

 - Assist in negotiating contracts with subcontractors and suppliers



Basic Qualifications

 - Minimum of 3 years of experience in earthworks or heavy civil projects

 - Proficiency in construction estimating software (e.g., Trimble, B2W, Agtek, or similar)

 - Proven track record of delivering accurate estimates and winning of complex construction projects

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams



Additional Requirements

 - Comfortable with working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX if not within commuting distance

 - Ability to travel to job sites across Texas



Compensation & Benefits

Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience and education. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. Our total compensation package also includes equity in TerraFirma. Relocation packages are available to help cover moving costs, if applicable.

source_jobs.title: Workplace & Office Coordinator
source_jobs.description:

Job Overview

You will be the person who makes our HQ work day to day. You will keep the space organized and stocked, coordinate vendors and repairs, manage shipping and receiving, and own IT procurement and equipment setup. You will also prepare the site for recruiting onsites and client tech demos and support team events and off-sites.



Responsibilities

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for our HQ and coordinate day-to-day site operations

 - Manage vendors across cleaning, maintenance, repairs, and security, and ensure issues are resolved quickly

 - Own shipping and receiving, including package intake, outbound shipments, and onsite deliveries

 - Maintain office and kitchen inventory, keep spaces stocked and organized, and run food programs for the team

 - Plan and execute internal events including team lunches, recruiting onsites, and holiday celebrations

 - Own end-to-end logistics for team off-sites including travel, venues, schedules, activities, and budgets

 - Procure and manage office IT equipment including laptops, monitors, peripherals, and basic inventory tracking

 - Ensure every new hire is set up on Day 1 with a ready workstation, access badges, and onboarding supplies

 - Troubleshoot basic workplace and equipment issues and escalate quickly when needed

 - Support leadership with ad-hoc administrative tasks and calendar coordination as required



Basic Qualifications

 - 3+ years of experience in office management, workplace operations, or executive administration

 - Demonstrated ability to manage vendors, budgets, and facility operations with high attention to detail

 - Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize multiple high-urgency tasks in parallel

 - Comfortable purchasing and managing IT equipment inventory and using modern collaboration tools

 - Experience planning events or off-sites for 50+ people

 - A high-agency, hands-on mindset with a “no task is too small” attitude



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience in a high-growth startup environment

 - Experience supporting engineering, hardware, or operations teams

 - Previous experience with IT procurement or basic IT support

 - Familiarity with safety and compliance considerations in a mixed office and workshop environment



Additional Requirements

 - Must be willing to work extended hours or weekends when needed to support critical events o

source_jobs.description:

 - B.S. in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field

 - 2+ years of professional or project experience as a full-stack or application developer, working across frontend, backend, and infrastructure

 - Proficiency in building interactive UIs using modern frameworks and platforms (e.g., React, Vue.js, Unity, Unreal, or equivalent)

 - A strong understanding of computer networking principles, including routing and protocols across the OSI model

 - Proficiency in developing software to run on Linux-based systems


Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience working with real-time data systems or low-latency communication protocols (e.g., WebRTC, gRPC)

 - Experience with data visualization, transformations, and working with 3D, geospatial, or LiDAR data

 - Experience developing for game engines (e.g., Unity, Un

source_jobs.title: Avionics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Job Overview

You will design and maintain the electrical systems that enable reliable remote operation of construction equipment. This includes wiring harnesses, circuits, and system integration for drive-by-wire machines in rugged outdoor environments. The role is hands-on, spanning fabrication, lab and field troubleshooting, and vendor collaboration to streamline builds.



Responsibilities

 - Engineering Design: Design wiring harnesses, circuits, and systems to enable reliable control of drive-by-wire machinery in high vibration, dirty, outdoor environments

 - DFM/DFT & Serviceability: Drive improvements to designs to simplify builds and installs, reduce wiring ambiguity, add practical test points, and make field service faster and more repeatable

 - Install & Integration Support: Support vehicle builds and retrofits by unblocking installation issues, improving harness routing/packaging, and iterating rapidly based on shop and field feedback

 - Documentation: Eliminate uncertainty through the use of effective documentation practices; create and maintain wiring diagrams, harness drawings, assembly/installation procedures, test procedures, bills of materials, and suitable alternates

 - Fabrication: Assemble and install harnessing and circuits for development testing and use in an operational environment out in the field

 - Debugging, Failure Analysis & Corrective Action: Use measurement equipment to troubleshoot electrical systems on the bench and in the field; own root-cause investigations, implement immediate fixes, and drive sustainable long-term solutions

 - Vendor / Contractor Collaboration: Develop and maintain working relationships with manufacturers to streamline production and improve build and install quality over time



Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Electrical or Computer Engineering

 - Practical experience working on electrical systems
   
   

Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Circuit design fundamentals and component selection experience

 - Experience designing and qualifying systems for high vibration and/or outdoor environments

 - Experience using electrical measuring equipment (oscilloscope, multimeter, signal analyzer, etc.)

 - Experience building wiring harnesses and assembling circuit boards (preferably to industry or other recognized standards)

 - Strong understanding of “fail-safe” design principles

 - Experience designing, setting up and debugging computer networks (Ethernet, CAN bus, Cellular, WIFI, etc.)

 - Experience creating clear installation/test documentation for technicians and partners (photos, diagrams, checklists, acceptance tests)



Additional Requirements

 - Comfortable with working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX if not within commuting distance

 - Must be willing to work extended hours or weekends when needed to support critical deadlines


Compensation & Benefits

Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience and education. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. Our total compensation package also includes equity in TerraFirma. Relocation packages are available to help cover moving costs, if applicable.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications,

source_jobs.title: Brand & Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

We are looking for a Brand and Marketing Lead to own how TerraFirma shows up in the world. You will define the narrative, own the marketing budget, build and manage a creative ecosystem, and orchestrate how our story is told across channels and major moments.

You will set the strategy, hire and manage contractors and agencies, and drive the rollout of major company moments such as fundraises, product launches, partnerships, and flagship projects. While you should have strong creative instincts and taste, this is not a hands-on videographer or editor role. Your leverage comes from direction, coordination, and execution at scale.

You will work directly with the CEO to position TerraFirma as the category-defining company in construction automation, attracting top tier talent, partners, customers, and investors.



Responsibilities

 - Own TerraFirma’s brand, narrative, and channel strategy, ensuring consistency and a high quality bar

 - Own the marketing budget and build the function through contractors, agencies, and eventual hires

 - Plan and execute major announcements and launches across press, social, and owned channels

 - Drive CEO and founder amplification through podcasts, interviews, speaking, and earned media

 - Translate technical work into stories that attract talent, partners, customers, and investors



Basic Qualifications

 - 3 or more years of experience in brand, marketing, creative direction, communications, or related roles

 - Proven experience owning brand or marketing strategy end to end

 - Experience managing budgets and external partners (agencies, contractors, freelancers)

 - Demonstrated success launching campaigns, announcements, or brands

 - Strong understanding of modern media, social platforms, and distribution

 - Experience working in or adjacent to technical, industrial, or hardware driven companies



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience in robotics, construction, aerospace, defense, or deep tech

 - A strong network across media, creators, and brand agencies

 - Ability to direct production without needing to personally shoot or edit

 - Evidence of building cultural relevance around technical products



Additional Requirements

 - Must be willing to work on site in Austin, TX

 - Comfortable operating in a fast paced, scrappy startup environment

 - Willing to work irregular hours around launches, ann

source_jobs.title: Robotics Software Engineer - Motion Planning, State Estimation, & Control
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

You will design and deploy the algorithms that enable heavy machinery to perform complex tasks with advanced automation in dynamic, unstructured environments. Your focus will span state estimation, motion and trajectory planning, and control, taking ideas from simulation through to real-world deployment on multi-ton robotic platforms. You will architect systems that fuse data from diverse sensors, optimize movement on challenging job sites, and execute with reliability under real-time constraints.



Responsibilities

 - State Estimation & Sensor Fusion – Design, implement, and deploy real‑time estimation pipelines that remain robust to sensor noise, vibration, and bias

 - Motion / Trajectory / Path Planning – Create high‑level planners and low‑level controllers that optimize for safety, efficiency, and task completion

 - Software & System Architecture – Own C++/Python codebases, and MCU firmware that realize Guidance, Navigation & Control functions on production hardware

 - Simulation & Tooling – Generate digital twins, Hardware-in-the-Loop setups, and interactive visualizations to validate algorithms before field trials

 - Field Deployment – Bring algorithms onto machines, run field experiments, diagnose issues, and iterate rapidly
   
   

Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Robotics, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Aerospace Engineering, or a related field

 - 2+ years of professional or advanced research experience delivering robotic software from first‑principles modelling through hardware deployment

 - Proven track record owning large deliverables and driving multi‑disciplinary roadmaps



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Hands‑on deployment of Kalman filters or equivalent estimators on real‑world platforms (e.g., drones, tractors, autonomous vehicles, aircraft, rockets)

 - Deep familiarity with sensor‑fusion toolchains and libraries

 - Expertise in different motion planning frameworks

 - Strong C++17/C and Python skills on Linux

 - Background integrating and calibrating sensors such as IMUs, RTK‑GPS, wheel/arm encoders, LiDAR, and cameras



Additional Requirements

 - Must be willing to work extended hours or weekends when needed to support critical deadlines or triage urgent deployment issues

 - Comfortable with working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX if not within

Job Overview

Support a major U.S. Government program delivering remote-controlled heavy equipment for disaster response and recovery worldwide. You will keep the contract running smoothly by owning invoicing, compliance documentation, and government-required reports and deliverables. Your work helps ensure critical equipment can be deployed and sustained when and where it is needed most. You will work closely with the Program Director and coordinate with accounting and legal partners to ensure everything is accurate, complete, and delivered on time.



Responsibilities

 - Prepare and submit contract invoices on the required schedule, including supporting documents and follow-ups through payment

 - Track expenses and organize receipts and documentation needed for reimbursement and audits, including costs related to field deployment and response readiness

 - Coordinate government-required reports and deliverables by gathering inputs, assembling submissions, and tracking due dates and approvals, supporting time-sensitive disaster relief milestones

 - Work with external accountants and attorneys on billing structure, compliance questions, and contract administration needs

 - Maintain clean, audit-ready contract records, including submission logs, supporting documentation, and government feedback tracking

 - Build templates, trackers, and checklists that make invoicing, compliance, and deliverables simple and repeatable, especially for rapid-response operations



Qualifications & Requirements

 - 2+ years of experience in operations, coordination, finance or administration, or contracts work (government contract experience is a plus)

 - Highly organized and detail-oriented, with comfort owning deadlines and document-heavy workflows

 - Strong spreadsheet skills (Excel or Google Sheets) and ability to reconcile data across sources

 - Able to learn invoice and documentation requirements quickly and apply them consistently

 - Interest in supporting disaster relief, recovery, and mission-driven operations

 - Austin, TX (on-site). If not currently in Austin, relocation is available



Compensation & Benefits

Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience, demonstrated skills, and the scope of responsibilities for the role. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. This position includes a c

source_jobs.title: Program Director, Federal Construction & Operations
source_jobs.description:

Job Overview

Lead a major U.S. Government program delivering remote-controlled heavy equipment for high-impact construction and remediation missions around the world. You will lead global deployments and field execution, scaling a proven teleoperation capability into reliable operations across real-world sites.



Responsibilities

 - Own end-to-end program execution for a major U.S. Government contract, including scope, schedule, budget, compliance, and performance

 - Lead global field deployment for remote-controlled heavy equipment operations, including staffing, equipment staging, logistics, maintenance, and day-to-day operations across international sites

 - Serve as primary interface to U.S. Government stakeholders and partners and translate requirements into executable plans, milestones, and operational rhythms

 - Drive safety and risk management in hazardous environments and enforce a strong safety culture across all field activities

 - Build and lead multidisciplinary teams including operators, field engineers, technicians, and subcontractors with clear accountability and high execution standards

 - Own reporting cadence and performance tracking and drive continuous improvement based on lessons learned and government feedback



Qualifications & Requirements

 - 10+ years of experience leading large-scale construction, remediation, disaster relief, or heavy civil operations

 - Experience managing U.S. Government overseas construction or contingency operations such as DoW, USACE, DOS, USAID, or similar agencies

 - Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management, or related field

 - Ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. security clearance

 - Willing and able to spend significant time in Austin, TX (TerraFirma HQ) and travel internationally as needed, including extended in-country deployments



Compensation & Benefits

Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience, demonstrated skills, and the scope of responsibilities for the role. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. This position includes a competitive benefits package, which may include medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, and company holidays. Travel required for the role will be supported in accordance with company policy.

source_jobs.title: Heavy Equipment Operator - Heavy Civil & Excavation
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Job Overview
In this role, you will combine traditional heavy equipment operation with the use of advanced technologies like automation tools, tele-operation, and our proprietary Mission Control to execute earthworks involving clearing, grading, grubbing, and excavation.


Responsibilities

 - Operate advanced heavy equipment for site prep and excavation (e.g., excavators, bulldozers, graders, skid steers, etc.)

 - Learn how to use our proprietary Mission Control platform and on-site remote controlling technology

 - Perform daily equipment inspections, including onboard tech systems, and report maintenance needs through digital platforms

 - Execute excavation tasks while adhering to project specifications and safety protocols

 - Maintain a safe work environment by following OSHA regulations, company safety standards, and site-specific protocols

 - Assist with minor equipment troubleshooting and coordinate with maintenance partners for repairs
   
   

Basic Qualifications

 - Minimum 3 years of experience operating heavy equipment for site prep and excavation (e.g., excavators, bulldozers, graders)

 - Proven affinity with technological systems or use of construction software

 - High school diploma or equivalent



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Strong understanding of earthworks processes (e.g., grading, compaction, trenching)

 - High level of tech literacy and interest in new technologies (e.g., drones, simulators, or digital tools)

 - Ability to read and interpret digital site plans, blueprints, and grade stakes

 - Ability to work in various weather conditions, lift up to 50 lbs., and perform physically demanding tasks



Additional Requirements

 - Valid driver’s license; willingness to travel to project sites and work flexible hours, including weekends when required

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX, if not within commuting distance



Compensation & Benefits
Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience and education. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. Our total compensation package also includes equity in TerraFirma. Relocation packages are available to help cover moving costs, if applicable.

Job Overview

You will support the preparation of accurate cost estimates for earthworks and site development projects. Working closely with estimators, engineers, project managers, and business development, you will help turn project plans, field information, and pricing inputs into competitive bids.

This role is for someone early in their career who wants hands-on experience in heavy civil estimating. You will contribute to takeoffs, pricing, bid organization, subcontractor and supplier coordination, and estimating process improvement while building the foundation to grow into a stronger estimator.



Responsibilities

 - Assist in preparing quantity takeoffs and cost estimates for earthworks and site development projects, including materials, labor, equipment, and subcontracted scope

 - Review project plans, specifications, geotechnical reports, and bid documents to understand project scope and identify cost drivers

 - Collaborate with estimators, engineers, project managers, and business development managers to gather information necessary for accurate estimates

 - Support subcontractor and supplier outreach for pricing, scope clarification, and bid coverage

 - Help develop estimating standard operating procedures and templates that allow us to bid projects in a lean, fast, and low-risk manner

 - Participate in site visits, pre-bid walkthroughs, and customer interactions needed to scope and advance project bidding processes

 - Maintain organized documentation of estimates, takeoffs, pricing inputs, assumptions, and bid packages

 - Monitor project costs during construction and compare them to estimates to identify variances and improve future bids

 - Assist with bid leveling, scope comparisons, and internal estimate reviews



Basic Qualifications

 - 1+ years of experience in construction, civil engineering, estimating, earthworks, or heavy civil projects

 - Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, or related field preferred but not required

 - Familiarity with construction estimating software such as Trimble, B2W, AGTEK, Bluebeam, or similar tools is helpful but not required

 - Strong attention to detail and analytical skills

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams

 - Interest in heavy civil construction, estimating, and field operations

In this role, you will spearhead the planning, execution, and delivery of heavy civil projects (excavation, grading, site-prep, etc.), leveraging our innovative automation and mission control technology. This role is pivotal in overseeing the entire project lifecycle, ensuring projects are completed safely, meet quality expectations, stay on schedule, and remain within budget.



Responsibilities

 - Manage project planning, budgets, schedules, and resources, maximizing the efficiency gains from automated equipment to deliver cost-effective outcomes

 - Ensure strict compliance with safety regulations and industry standards, addressing the safety considerations of automated construction

 - Monitor project progress, oversee job site operations, proactively identify risks, and implement solutions to maintain project timelines and quality standards

 - Communicate regularly with clients, providing clear updates on project status, and milestones

 - Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, or a related field

 - Minimum of 2 years of experience in construction project management, with at least 1 year focused on earthworks or heavy civil construction

 - Proven track record of managing projects involving heavy equipment and earth-moving activities, with budgets ranging from $50,000 to $20 million

 - Strong understanding of earthworks construction techniques, materials, and equipment

 - Proficiency in project management software (e.g., Primavera, MS Project) and tools for scheduling and budgeting


Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience with construction automation, robotics, or advanced technology integration in project management

 - Exceptional leadership and team management skills, with a history of mentoring and developing talent

source_jobs.title: Radar Systems Engineer - Signal Processing
source_jobs.description:

Job Overview

In this role, you’ll take hands-on ownership of various systems spanning hardware and software, and analog and digital domains. Initially, you'll lead the development of a new DSP system from concept to functional prototype, owning the full hardware and software stack. Daily tasks will include designing and testing RF frontends, analyzing digital signals, running field tests, and creating algorithms to detect patterns and synthesize sensor data for extracting meaningful features from RF and EM interactions with the environment. Alongside building custom hardware, you’ll oversee validation and testing campaigns as needed.



Responsibilities

 - Own the system architecture for an electromagnetic sensing application from concept through first prototype

 - Design and validate new sensor hardware. Must be comfortable with RF circuit and antenna concepts and ready to dive into real-time FPGA processing

 - Develop, test, and deploy algorithms in realms of sensor fusion, image processing, and object detection in Python/R/C++. Be ready to explore reinforced learning for a higher degree of confidence

 - Work with interdisciplinary teams to design, test, and deliver custom hardware and software solutions

 - Take on leadership roles when required, mentor new employees, and actively participate in the hiring process



Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a closely related field

 - 2+ years of professional or project experience with RF, DSP, or electrical engineering with hands-on hardware development and prototyping

 - Proficiency in fundamentals of signals analysis and electrical engineering

 - Demonstrated experience owning large-scale deliverables, leading teams, and driving product roadmaps



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Strong background in digital and image signal processing, machine learning, and efficient manipulation of large datasets for high-throughput analysis

 - Experience designing or testing radar systems including antennas/frontends targeting the VHF and UHF bands

 - Experience with FPGAs, including hardware design, HDL programming, and common low-level and embedded protocols

 - Experience prototyping with SDR and open source tools like GNU Radio

 - PCB design experience for rapid prototyping, including schematic capture and layout

 - Familiarity with C/C++, Python/R, and Linux systems

source_jobs.title: Takeoff Technician – Earthworks & Site Development
source_jobs.description:

Job Overview

You will produce the quantity takeoffs that drive TerraFirma’s estimating process for earthworks and site development projects. You will work closely with estimators, engineers, and project managers to translate civil plans and field conditions into accurate, decision-ready quantities that shape how we bid work.

This role is highly detail-oriented and execution-focused. You will own takeoff outputs across grading, excavation, utilities, paving, and related scopes, while helping improve the speed, accuracy, and repeatability of how TerraFirma prices jobs. As our internal estimating tools and workflows evolve, you will also help refine the systems that make takeoff faster and more reliable.



Responsibilities

 - Prepare detailed quantity takeoffs for earthworks and site development scopes, including grading, excavation, fill, utilities, and paving

 - Review civil drawings, specifications, geotechnical reports, and related bid documents to define scope accurately

 - Generate cut/fill quantities, haul assumptions, and material volume calculations that can be translated directly into estimates

 - Work closely with estimators to structure takeoff outputs into clear, usable estimating inputs

 - Identify scope gaps, quantity risks, and plan inconsistencies early in the bid process

 - Support pre-bid planning through site visits, walkthroughs, and coordination with project stakeholders when required

 - Maintain organized, traceable documentation for takeoffs, assumptions, and bid packages

 - Improve internal templates, standards, and workflows to increase takeoff speed, consistency, and accuracy

 - Provide feedback on software tools and internal systems used in estimating and project planning



Basic Qualifications

 - 2–5 years of experience performing construction takeoffs, preferably in earthworks, grading, site development, or heavy civil construction

 - Strong experience with takeoff or earthwork software such as AGTEK, Trimble Business Center, B2W Estimate, Bluebeam, Civil 3D, or similar tools

 - Ability to read and interpret civil engineering plans, specifications, and supporting project documents

 - Strong understanding of grading plans, cut/fill analysis, and site development scope

 - High attention to detail and strong analytical skills

 - Ability to work effectively with estimators, engineers, and project teams in a fast-moving envir

source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager - Heavy Civil & Excavation
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.


Our Story & Mission
At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.


Job Overview
In this role, you will drive TerraFirma's efforts in forging strategic alliances and securing new business opportunities. This role focuses on building relationships with developers, contractors, and other major players in the earthmoving industry while driving the sales pipeline for site prep and excavation projects.


Responsibilities

 - Develop and maintain strategic partnerships with major developers, general contractors, corporates, and other major players in the earthmoving industry

 - Drive the development of TerraFirma’s network of partners at the forefront of building out the next generation of infrastructure in the US

 - Represent TerraFirma at industry events, conferences, and networking opportunities to promote our brand and services

 - Lead and coordinate the preparation and submission of proposals, bids, and tenders, collaborating with estimators and project teams to ensure accurate and compelling submissions

 - Support the development and promotion of marketing materials and case studies showcasing successful projects and partnerships



Basic Qualifications

 - 5+ years in business development, sales, or partnership management in construction, excavation, or related industries

 - Familiarity with CRM software (e.g., Salesforce), proposal writing tools, and proven understanding of earthworks or heavy equipment operations a plus

 - Proven experience with business development in frontier construction technology and robotics



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Extended network in the Texas earthworks, construction, and data center industries

 - Demonstrated success in securing contracts and building long-term partnerships, with a portfolio of at least 5 major deals in the past 3 years

 - Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills



Additional Requirements

 - Must be willing to work extended hours or weekends when needed to support critical deadlines or triage urgent deployment issues

 - Comfortable with working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX if not within commuting distance



Compensation & Benefits
Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience and education. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. Our total compensation package also includes equity in TerraFirma. Relocation packages ar

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At TerraFirma, we’re redefining how the world moves earth for construction. Founded by former SpaceX engineers and backed by Bain Capital Ventures, TerraFirma is automating construction to make it faster, cheaper, and safer.



Our Story & Mission

At SpaceX, we were part of the teams that built the largest rocket in human history, launched the largest constellation of satellites ever made, and delivered connectivity to millions of people worldwide. What we learned was simple: infrastructure is everything.

At Starbase, the biggest barrier to launching missions to Mars was not building the rocket. It was how slow, expensive, and complex it was to build the infrastructure around it. We lacked buildings, roads, water, power, communications, and waste management, and constructing them took far more time and resources than it should have.

From a first-principles perspective, that doesn't make sense. It shouldn't be this hard or costly to create the foundations for progress. That’s when we realized something important: the same engineering mindset that enabled us to tackle space exploration and mass manufacturing could be applied to infrastructure. By bringing the principles of simplification, iteration, acceleration, and automation to construction, we can unlock a new era in how the world builds.

Accelerating infrastructure unlocks progress across every domain, creating a self-sustaining engine for tackling humanity’s toughest challenges. The innovations we develop on Earth will lay the groundwork for the technologies that one day make settlement on Mars possible.



Job Overview

You will design and maintain the electrical systems that enable reliable remote operation of construction equipment. This includes wiring harnesses, circuits, and system integration for drive-by-wire machines in rugged outdoor environments. The role is hands-on, spanning fabrication, lab and field troubleshooting, and vendor collaboration to streamline builds.



Responsibilities

 - Design wiring harnesses, circuits, and systems to enable safe and reliable control of drive-by-wire machinery in high vibration, dirty, outdoor environments

 - Develop and maintain working relationships with manufacturers to streamline production and manufacturing of designs

 - Assemble and install harnessing and circuits for development testing and use in an operational environment out in the field

 - Use measurement equipment to troubleshoot electrical systems on the bench and in the field. Use the tools available to implement immediate fixes and develop sustainable long-term solutions

 - Eliminate uncertainty through the use of effective documentation practices and facilitate collaboration and future development



Basic Qualifications

 - B.S. in Electrical Engineering

 - Practical experience working on electrical systems



Preferred Skills & Experience

 - Experience building wiring harnesses and assembling circuit boards (preferably to industry or other recognized standards)

 - Experience using electrical measuring equipment (oscilloscope, multimeter, signal analyzer, etc.)

 - Experience designing and qualifying systems for high vibration and/or outdoor environments

 - Strong understanding of "fail-safe" design principles



Additional Requirements

 - Comfortable with working in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Must be willing to relocate to Austin, TX if not within commuting distance

 - Must be willing to work extended hours or weekends when needed to support critical deadlines


Compensation & Benefits

Base compensation will vary based on factors such as relevant experience and education. Salaries and leveling will be determined on a case-by-case basis if an offer is extended. Our total compensation package also includes equity in TerraFirma. Relocation packages are available to help cover moving costs, if applicable.

notes: Highly technical roles, former SpaceX engineers, and automation-focused construction company
